X-H2S COMPANION

Tutorial · Presets · SOS · Manual · Exercises

Troubleshooting — by symptom

Shot is too dark or too brightCOMMON
  1. Check exposure correction: look for the ±0.0 value on the display. Does it say e.g. −2? Turn it back with the rear command dial. (C2 is deliberately set to +1!)
  2. Backlight (subject in front of a bright window/the sun)? Push correction to +1/+2, or change your position.
  3. Note: because of Auto Update, a changed correction stays saved in the preset — reset it yourself.
Everything looks blurry in the viewfinderCOMMON

If photos look sharp on the rear screen but the viewfinder itself looks mushy: turn the diopter wheel to the left of the viewfinder until the overlaid text is tack-sharp. It tends to get bumped while being carried.

Focus is off / the wrong spot is sharpCOMMON
  1. Check the focus-mode switch on the front of the camera: is it on M? Then nothing focuses automatically → switch to S (still) or C (moving).
  2. Nudge the focus point onto the subject with the joystick. Press the joystick in to snap the point back to center.
  3. For people/animals: is a detection box visible? If not: Fn2 toggles eye/face detection, Fn1 toggles animal detection. The subject type (animal/bird/…) is set in the Q menu.
  4. Too close? Every lens has a minimum focus distance — take a step back.
Camera won't take the shotCOMMON
  1. Any message on the display? CARD FULL / NO CARD → see the memory-card entries below.
  2. AF-C may still be waiting for focus lock: reposition briefly, half-press, then press fully.
  3. Battery nearly empty? Swap or charge it.
  4. Nothing helps: turn the camera OFF → wait 10 seconds → ON.
“CARD FULL” — memory card is full
  1. Insert a spare card (door on the right) — the simplest fix.
  2. Good to know: photos are written as a backup to both cards (CFexpress + SD) — both fill up in parallel, and the full one stops shooting. So swap/clear specifically the full card. Videos live only on the CFexpress card.
  3. Or sort through and delete: playback → trash button → IMAGE → delete individual images.
  4. FORMAT erases EVERYTHING irreversibly — only use it once your images are backed up.
“CARD ERROR” / card not recognized
  1. Turn the camera off, remove the card, check the contacts (clean? dry?), reinsert until it clicks.
  2. Try a second card — if the camera reads that one fine, the first card is the problem: stop writing to it, recover the images on a computer later.
  3. Is the write-protect switch on the SD card set to “unlocked”?
Battery dead / won't charge
  • Charging works directly over USB-C in the camera — a power bank works too (ideally one with USB-PD). A light on the camera shows charging is active.
  • Cold drains batteries fast: keep a spare battery warm against your body (an inside jacket pocket).
  • The viewfinder/screen use the most power — turn the camera off during breaks.
Something's off / the camera is behaving oddlyCOMMON
  1. Press the Q button and scan the tiles — what looks unusual? Fix it right there.
  2. Careful: turning the dial away and back does NOT help — this camera saves changes into the preset automatically (Auto Update). Reset the values you want yourself.
  3. Lost in the menu? Press DISP/BACK a few times.
  4. Bigger mess? The intended preset values are on the “My Setup” page; restore a settings backup via the XApp (“Connection” page).
  5. Last resort: turn the camera off, pull the battery for 10 seconds, put it back in.
I'm recording video but wanted a photo (or the other way round)

The STILL/MOVIE switch sits top left, under the mode dial. STILL = photo, MOVIE = video. Both worlds have their own menus and their own C presets — which is why “everything suddenly looks different”.

Shots have suddenly turned black & white

The dial is on C1 (Acros = the black & white preset), or the film simulation got changed (check the Q menu — it stays changed because of Auto Update!). Turn the dial to C2/C3, or reset the simulation. RAW files, by the way, always keep all the colors — here's how to bring them back in-camera afterwards.

Screen is black / viewfinder stays off
  1. The eye sensor at the viewfinder switches the screen off as soon as something gets close (a finger, a strap, a rain jacket!). Give it some room.
  2. Press the VIEW MODE button at the viewfinder until “EVF/LCD Auto” or similar is selected.
  3. DISP/BACK cycles the display variants — maybe “screen off” is simply the active one.
Overheating icon while filming (yellow/red)

Can happen during long 4K recordings (especially 100p) in the sun. Yellow = early warning, red = shutdown coming soon.

  • Pause recording, move the camera into the shade, turn it off.
  • Flip the screen away from the body (better cooling), shoot shorter clips.
Date/time is wrong (travel time zone)

MENU/OK → wrench icon → USER SETTING → TIME DIFFERENCE → choose LOCAL TIME and set the destination zone. Switch back to HOME TIME once you're back. (With the XApp paired, your phone can set the time too.)

Photos won't transfer to my phone / no geo-tagging

Both run through the FUJIFILM XApp — see the “Connection” page → “Phone setup” for how to set it up. Short version:

  1. Transfer: select and send images from playback, or turn on automatic transfer in the app.
  2. Geo-tagging: enable location sync in the XApp + on the camera GEOTAGGING → ON (in the Network/USB menu). Leave Bluetooth on on your phone.
Rain, spray, dust

The body is weather-sealed — light rain is fine, as long as the lens is also WR-sealed (does “WR” appear in the lens name?). Wipe drops off promptly with a microfiber cloth, only swap lenses somewhere dry, and never zip a wet camera into a closed bag.

None of this helping?

Official troubleshooting: on the “Manual” page tap “Troubleshooting” (p. 358) — or search there for the keyword.
